concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Measures the amount of memory an algorithm uses relative to the size of its input.

concepts.name: Auxiliary Space
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory used by an algorithm beyond the input storage.

concepts.name: Input Space
concepts.short_definition: The amount of memory required to store the input to an algorithm.

concepts.name: Total Space
concepts.short_definition: The sum of input space and auxiliary space used by an algorithm.

concepts.name: Constant Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that uses a fixed amount of memory, regardless of input size.

concepts.name: Linear Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows linearly with the input size.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows logarithmically with the input size.

concepts.name: Quadratic Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows quadratically with the input size.

concepts.name: Exponential Space
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose memory usage grows exponentially with the input size.

concepts.name: Big O Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe the limiting behavior of a function when the argument tends towards a particular value or infinity.

concepts.name: Variables
concepts.short_definition: Named storage locations that hold values, contributing to memory usage.

concepts.name: Data Structures
concepts.short_definition: Ways of organizing and storing data, each with its own memory footprint.

concepts.name: Arrays
concepts.short_definition: Contiguous blocks of memory storing elements of the same type.

concepts.name: Linked Lists
concepts.short_definition: Data structures where elements are linked by pointers, requiring memory for nodes and pointers.

concepts.name: Stacks
concepts.short_definition: Data structures following a Last-In, First-Out principle, often implemented with arrays or linked lists.

concepts.name: Queues
concepts.short_definition: Data structures following a First-In, First-Out principle, often implemented with arrays or linked lists.

concepts.name: Trees
concepts.short_definition: Non-linear data structures with a root node and child nodes, requiring memory for nodes and pointers.

concepts.name: Graphs
concepts.short_definition: Data structures representing relationships between objects, often using adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Hash Tables
concepts.short_definition: Data structures that map keys to values, often using arrays and hash functions.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique where a function calls itself, contributing to call stack space.

concepts.name: Call Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores information about active subroutines (function calls).

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.short_definition: A programming technique that repeats a block of code, generally with lower space overhead than recursion.

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.short_definition: The process of reserving memory for data during program execution.

concepts.name: Static Allocation
concepts.short_definition: Memory allocated at compile time, with a fixed size.

concepts.name: Dynamic Allocation
concepts.short_definition: Memory allocated during program execution, which can vary in size.

concepts.name: Input Size
concepts.short_definition: The measure of the input data that an algorithm processes, often used as a variable in complexity analysis.

concepts.name: Worst-Case Space
concepts.short_definition: The maximum amount of memory an algorithm might use for any input of a given size.

concepts.name: Average-Case Space
concepts.short_definition: The expected amount of memory an algorithm uses for a typical input of a given size.

concepts.name: Best-Case Space
concepts.short_definition: The minimum amount of memory an algorithm might use for any input of a given size.

concepts.name: Space-Time Tradeoff
concepts.short_definition: The concept that an algorithm can sometimes reduce its execution time by using more memory, or vice versa.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: Measures the amount of time an algorithm takes to run relative to the size of its input.

concepts.name: Primitive Data Types
concepts.short_definition: Fundamental data types like integers, floats, booleans, which typically occupy a fixed amount of memory.

concepts.name: Objects
concepts.short_definition: Data structures that can hold multiple fields, contributing to memory usage based on their structure.

concepts.name: Pointers
concepts.short_definition: Variables that store memory addresses, used extensively in linked data structures.

concepts.name: Memory Leaks
concepts.short_definition: A type of resource leak that occurs when a computer program incorrectly manages memory allocations, causing it to consume more and more memory over time.

concepts.name: Garbage Collection
concepts.short_definition: Automatic memory management that reclaims memory occupied by objects that are no longer in use by the application.

concepts.name: Bit
concepts.short_definition: The smallest unit of data in computing, representing either a 0 or a 1.

concepts.name: Byte
concepts.short_definition: A unit of digital information that most commonly consists of eight bits.

concepts.name: Memory Address
concepts.short_definition: A unique identifier for a location in computer memory.
